_comment: Generic Spring Boot Java 21 + Python 3 egg. Application supplies JAR and manages its own Python deps at runtime.
name: Spring Boot (Java 21 + Python)
author: you@example.com
description: Run a Spring Boot fat JAR with Java 21 and Python 3 on PATH. Upload your JAR to /home/container. The application is responsible for cloning or vendoring any Python projects it needs.

installation.script:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
cd /mnt/server
mkdir -p data
cat > README.txt <<'EOF'
Spring Boot (Java 21 + Python)

1. Upload your fat JAR (default name: app.jar).
2. Upload any companion files your app expects (e.g. worker scripts).
3. Start the server from the panel.

Runtime image includes: Java 21, python3, pip, git.
Your Spring Boot app should install or clone Python dependencies at startup if needed.

Port: set SERVER_PORT in the panel allocation; startup passes --server.port.
EOF
echo "Install complete."

variables.name: JAR file name
variables.description: The Spring Boot fat JAR to run (in /home/container).

variables.name: Python binary
variables.description: Python executable on PATH for apps that shell out to Python.
